item.title: Iran’s Potential Peace Negotiator Known for Strongman Approach
item.description: As US President Donald Trump doubles down on his claim that he’s negotiating with Iran to end the war in the Persian Gulf, a conservative politician of the Islamic Republic has emerged as his most likely counterpart.

item.title: Barclays Pulls Back on Asset-Based Lending After MFS, Tricolor
item.description: Barclays Plc is scaling back its asset-based lending to smaller borrowers, according to people with knowledge of the matter, after the collapses of Market Financial Solutions Ltd. and Tricolor Holdings left the firm facing losses.

item.title: AI Notetaker Granola Hits $1.5 Billion Value in $125 Million Funding
item.description: Granola Inc., a startup whose artificial intelligence software has been gaining traction as a notetaking tool among Silicon Valley workers, is raising $125 million at a $1.5 billion valuation, led by Index Ventures.

item.title: China’s Exports Stay Strong as AI Boom Offsets War Headwinds
item.description: Demand for AI gear helped buoy global goods trade last year, and the momentum looks solid this year too

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Arm, Braze, Chewy, GM, KB Home, Terns
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.9% as of 7:55 a.m. in New York as the Trump administration pushes for talks with Iran.

item.title: UAE’s Alterra Joins General Atlantic in Tech Deal Amid Iran War
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s Alterra is backing a General Atlantic unit’s investment in a technology platform, the latest sign that Middle East investors are continuing with dealmaking even as the Iran war approaches the one-month mark.

item.title: CoreWeave Shares Lag Rival Nebius in Wild Year Since IPO
item.description: One year after CoreWeave Inc.’s tumultuous initial public offering the stock is being trounced by rival neocloud provider Nebius Group NV.

item.title: Asia Braces for Worst-Case Energy Scenarios as Iran War Drags On
item.description: Governments across Asia are preparing for worst-case energy scenarios that could include a prolonged and severe disruption to supplies, even as the US draws up a plan to end the war in Iran.

item.title: Hedge Fund Boss Builds ‘Ignore Trump’ Strategy for Oil Stocks
item.description: Anaconda Invest SA, a boutique hedge fund focused on energy, says it’s decided to disregard statements by US President Donald Trump about the Iran war, in an effort to avoid being distracted by an excess of signals.

item.title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Sees S&P Profit Boom Despite Iran War
item.description: Even as war in the Middle East roiled markets this month, some investors are finding solace in Corporate America’s growth machine, which not only remains intact — but is showing signs of thriving.

item.title: Danone Leads Charge of European Bond Deals as Market Opens Up
item.description: Yogurt maker Danone SA is leading a flurry of activity in European bond markets as borrowers jump on more stable market conditions, sparked by a renewed push to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Adani Eyes Partnerships With Meta, Google Amid Data Center Boom
item.description: Indian billionaire Gautam Adani is in talks with American technology giants including Meta Platforms Inc. and Google for partnerships in his fast-expanding data center business, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Hoarding Threatens to Worsen Fuel and Fertilizer Crunch From Iran War
item.description: By restricting free trade, governments risk inadvertently making a bad situation worse.

item.title: Central Banks’ Dilemma on Iran War Inflation: Look Through or Act Fast
item.description: Australia’s and New Zealand’s central bank chiefs outline alternative approaches to dealing with Iran War inflation pressures

item.title: AI Has Already Changed Software Contracts: They’re Shorter
item.description: Customers fear long-term deals will leave them with obsolete products

item.title: US Mortgage Rates Jump Further to Five-Month High of 6.43%
item.description: US mortgage rates climbed for a third straight week, pushing home-financing costs to the highest since October and dealing a blow to both purchasing and refinancing activity.

item.title: Norway Set to Move Away From Interest-Rate Cuts
item.description: Norway’s central bank will probably pivot from prospective interest-rate reductions this week as war in the Middle East adds to officials’ concerns over stubbornly high inflation.

item.title: Temu-Owner PDD’s Profit Slides 11% After China Consumption Wanes
item.description: PDD Holdings Inc. posted disappointing earnings and warned that growing investments in logistics will hurt its performance.

item.title: World Bank Says Mozambique Deficits Risk $50 Billion LNG Project
item.description: The World Bank warned Mozambique’s current economic trajectory puts at risk the $50 billion in gas projects the government is counting on to help bolster its flagging economy, in one of the starkest warnings yet on the nation’s persistent debt-fueled overspending.

item.title: Europe Faced With Near-Empty Gas Stores Just as War Hits Supply
item.description: Europe is about to start the gas stockpiling season with key storage tanks depleted, meaning it will need to compete even more with Asian buyers to secure supplies, just as the Middle East conflict disrupts energy flows.

item.title: Retail Investors Are Showing Signs of Fatigue as Stocks Falter
item.description: The most reliable US stock buyers turned into sellers this week.

item.title: Iran Demands Crew, Cargo Details to Send Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: Vessels seeking to transit the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian protection are being asked to provide lists of crew and cargo, along with voyage details and bills of lading, in order to secure a green light from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

item.title: Oil Theft Is Burning a Billion-Dollar Hole in West Texas’ Economy
item.description: When it was $65 a barrel, crude was already going missing from Permian oil fields at record rates. Now that it’s almost $100, authorities are bracing for more pain.

item.title: Riksbank Minutes Show Commitment to Steady Rate Despite Iran War
item.description: Officials at the Riksbank believe steady Swedish borrowing costs are keeping inflation on track to their 2% target even with the risk of higher price growth triggered by the Iran war, the minutes of the latest policy meeting showed.

item.title: Ex-Houlihan Tech Bankers Launch Digital Infrastructure Fund
item.description: Two London-based technology bankers at Houlihan Lokey Inc. have left the advisory firm to launch a fund investing in the booming market for digital infrastructure.

item.title: Lanxess Soars as JPMorgan’s Double Upgrade Provides Rare Boost
item.description: Lanxess AG shares soared after JPMorgan Chase & Co. double upgraded the German specialty chemical company, saying it should get an earnings boost as the war in Iran eases competitive pressures and increases pricing power.

item.title: Hungary Touts Yuan Swap Line as Orban Seeks Chinese Financing
item.description: The Hungarian central bank’s swap line with the People’s Bank of China provides a source of stability to the nation’s funding amid market volatility, according to a senior official.

item.title: Fosun Resort Chain Club Med Is Said to Consider Hong Kong for IPO
item.description: Club Med SAS is considering an initial public offering and is looking at Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: Turkey Is Urging Gulf Arab States Not to Join War Against Iran
item.description: Turkey is conducting intensive diplomatic efforts to try and prevent Gulf Arab countries from joining the US-Israeli war against Iran, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Signs of Jet Fuel Hoarding Emerge in Asia on Iran Oil Shock
item.description: Signs are growing that Asian countries are hoarding jet fuel after the Iran war sent oil prices surging, reflecting growing strain on the aviation industry.
